title: Methods of making metal bond abrasive articles and metal bond abrasive articles
abstract: A metal bond abrasive article comprises an abrasive member comprising a metal matrix containing abrasive particles; and a mounting member, wherein the mounting member extends into, and is directly fused to, the abrasive member. A method of making the metal bond abrasive particle involving powder bed binder jetting rapid prototyping is also disclosed.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of making a metal bond abrasive article, the method comprising the sequential steps: a) a subprocess comprising sequentially: i) depositing a layer of loose powder particles in a confined region, wherein the layer of loose powder particles comprises metal particles and abrasive particles, and wherein the layer of loose powder particles has substantially uniform thickness; ii) jetting a liquid binder precursor material onto at least one predetermined region of the layer of loose powder particles; iii) converting the liquid binder precursor material into a binder material that bonds together the layer of loose powder particles in the at least one predetermined region to form a layer of bonded powder particles; b) independently carrying out step a) a plurality of times to generate an abrasive preform comprising the layer of bonded powder particles, wherein the abrasive preform comprises at least one opening extending into the abrasive preform; c) separating substantially all of any loose powder particles from the abrasive preform; d) inserting a mounting member at least partially into the opening; e) sintering at least a portion of the metal particles in the abrasive preform while the mounting member is at least partially inserted in the opening to form the metal bond abrasive article. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the mounting member is securely affixed in the metal bond abrasive article after step e) is finished. 3. The method of claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the volume of the abrasive preform shrinks by at least 25 percent in step e). 4. The method of any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein in each step a), the loose powder particles are independently selected, and the liquid binder precursor material is independently selected. 5. The method of any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the abrasive particles comprise at least one of diamond particles or cubic boron nitride particles. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the abrasive particles comprise metal oxide ceramic particles. 7. The method of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the metal bond abrasive article includes at least one cooling channel. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the metal bond abrasive article comprises an abrasive grinding bit or an abrasive wheel. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the metal bond abrasive article comprises at least a portion of a rotary dental tool. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the liquid binder precursor material comprises a liquid vehicle having a polymer dissolved therein. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the liquid vehicle predominantly comprises water. 12. The method of any one of claims 1 to 11, further comprising, between steps c) and d), burning off at least a portion of the binder material. 13. The method of any one of claims 1 to 12, wherein the loose powder particles further comprise fluxing agent particles. 14. A metal bond abrasive article made according to the method of any one of claims 1 to 13. 15. A metal bond abrasive article comprising: an abrasive member comprising a metal matrix containing abrasive particles; and a mounting member, wherein the mounting member extends into, and is directly fused to, the abrasive member. 16. The metal bond abrasive article of claim 15, wherein the abrasive particles comprise at least one of diamond particles or cubic boron nitride particles. 17. The metal bond abrasive article of claim 15 or claim 16, wherein the abrasive particles comprise metal oxide ceramic particles. 18. The metal bond abrasive article of any one of claims 15 to 17, wherein the metal bond abrasive article includes at least one cooling channel. 19. The metal bond abrasive article of any one of claims 15 to 18, wherein the metal bond abrasive article comprises an abrasive grinding bit or an abrasive wheel. 20. The metal bond abrasive article of any one of claims 15 to 18, wherein the metal bond abrasive article comprises at least a portion of a rotary dental tool.
